title: Kubernetes CrashLoopBackOff Detection
exclusions: Does not replace UC-3.2.1 restart-rate counters without waiting_reason semantics. Does not cover init-only failures (UC-3.2.30). Does not cover probe failure taxonomy without kubelet backoff state (UC-3.2.43). Does not prove image CVE or supply-chain compromise alone. Windows node pools with different event shapes need a forked SPL.
evidence: Saved search uc_3_2_10_crashloop_backoff, lookups k8s_workload_routing.csv and k8s_namespace_tier.csv with version hashes, weekly CSV export of alert rows to the evidence index, and dashboard panels tied to the closing table command.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab namespace run kubectl run clbo-lab --image=busybox:1.36 --restart=Always -- sh -c 'exit 1', wait for kube-state-metrics to expose waiting_reason CrashLoopBackOff, execute uc_3_2_10_crashloop_backoff, and expect a row with time_in_clbo_min growing and severity at least high when workload_tier=prod in the tier lookup for that namespace.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Deploy nginx:stable with a valid ConfigMap and readiness probe in the same lab; confirm waiting_reason never stays CrashLoopBackOff beyond one scrape interval and the saved search yields no qualifying row for that pod across thirty minutes.

dataSources: index=k8s_metrics or index=k8s with sourcetype=prometheus:scrape:metrics or kube:objects:metrics carrying kube-state-metrics families kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason{reason="CrashLoopBackOff"}, kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_reason, kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_exitcode (label shape varies by scrape version), kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total, kube_pod_info, kube_pod_owner; index=k8s sourcetype=kube:events for Reason BackOff, Failed, Created, Killing, Pulled; index=k8s sourcetype=kube:container:logs or kubernetes:container:logs or OTLP-normalized container logs under k8s for stderr tails; index=k8s_audit sourcetype=kube:audit for create/patch/delete correlation; Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Kubernetes DaemonSet plus cluster receiver, or Splunk Connect for Kubernetes (legacy HEC) with object and metrics inputs. EKS control plane adds CloudWatch container logs path optionally; GKE workload logging and AKS Azure Monitor export the same kubelet semantics with different metadata wrappers.
app: Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector for Kubernetes (Splunk Observability / HEC exporters) with prometheus receiver or kubeletstats plus k8s_cluster receiver; kube-state-metrics Deployment and RBAC ClusterRole per upstream; Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitor when metrics ride through in-cluster Prometheus; Splunk Connect for Kubernetes (Splunk Add-on for Kubernetes documentation) for object and metrics HEC routes; Splunk Enterprise 9.2+ or Splunk Cloud search tier for scheduled alerts.
spl:
`comment("UC-3.2.10 K8s CrashLoopBackOff. Tunables: indexes k8s_metrics k8s; sourcetypes prometheus:scrape:metrics kube:objects:metrics kube:events; lookups k8s_workload_routing.csv (namespace,pod,owner_workload,on_call_team) k8s_namespace_tier.csv (namespace,workload_tier); earliest=-4h@m latest=now")`
| search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval metric_nm=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, MetricName, ""))))
| rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<ns_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "container=\"(?<ctr_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "reason=\"(?<reason_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "node=\"(?<node_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "image=\"(?<img_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "image_id=\"(?<image_id_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "\\s(?<metric_val>[0-9]+(?:\\.[0-9]+)?)\\s*$"
| eval namespace=trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, kubernetes_namespace, k8s_namespace, ns_rx, "")))
| eval pod=trim(toString(coalesce(pod, pod_name, k8s_pod, pod_rx, "")))
| eval container=trim(toString(coalesce(container, container_name, ctr_rx, "")))
| eval waiting_reason=trim(toString(coalesce(reason, waiting_reason, reason_rx, "")))
| eval node=trim(toString(coalesce(node, node_name, host_node, node_rx, "")))
| eval image=trim(toString(coalesce(image, img_rx, image_id_rx, "")))
| where (like(metric_nm, "%kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason%") OR like(_raw, "kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason")) AND waiting_reason="CrashLoopBackOff"
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod, container [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
    | rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<namespace>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "container=\"(?<container>[^\"]+)\""
    | where like(_raw, "kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total")
    | rex field=_raw "\\s(?<rv>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
    | eval r=tonumber(rv, 10)
    | stats earliest(r) AS r0 latest(r) AS r1 BY namespace, pod, container
    | eval restarts_24h=round(r1-r0, 0)
    | fields namespace, pod, container, restarts_24h ]
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod [
    search index=k8s sourcetype="kube:events" earliest=-4h@m latest=now ("BackOff" OR "Failed" OR "CrashLoopBackOff")
    | eval pod_ev=trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.name, involvedObject_name, pod, "")))
    | eval ns_ev=trim(toString(coalesce(metadata.namespace, namespace, "")))
    | stats latest(message) AS last_msg values(message) AS msg_mv BY ns_ev, pod_ev
    | eval recent_event_summary=substr(mvjoin(msg_mv, " | "), 1, 480)
    | rename ns_ev AS namespace pod_ev AS pod
    | fields namespace, pod, recent_event_summary ]
| sort 0 + namespace, pod, container, _time
| streamstats current=f last(waiting_reason) AS prev_reason BY namespace, pod, container
| eval clbo_on=if(waiting_reason="CrashLoopBackOff", 1, 0)
| eval clbo_enter=if(clbo_on==1 AND (prev_reason!="CrashLoopBackOff" OR isnull(prev_reason)), _time, null())
| streamstats last(clbo_enter) AS clbo_since BY namespace, pod, container
| eval time_in_clbo_min=if(clbo_on==1 AND isnotnull(clbo_since), round((now()-clbo_since)/60, 2), null())
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod, container [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<namespace>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "container=\"(?<container>[^\"]+)\""
    | where like(_raw, "kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_reason") OR like(lower(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, "")), "last_terminated_reason")
    | rex field=_raw "reason=\"(?<last_term_reason>[^\"]+)\""
    | stats latest(last_term_reason) AS last_term_reason BY namespace, pod, container ]
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod, container [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<namespace>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "container=\"(?<container>[^\"]+)\""
    | where like(_raw, "kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_exit_code") OR like(_raw, "last_terminated_exit")
    | rex field=_raw "\\s(?<exv>-?[0-9]+)\\s*$"
    | eval exit_code_last_term=tonumber(exv, 10)
    | stats latest(exit_code_last_term) AS exit_code_last_term BY namespace, pod, container ]
| join type=left max=0 namespace [
    | inputlookup k8s_namespace_tier.csv
    | eval namespace=trim(toString(namespace))
    | eval workload_tier=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(workload_tier, env_tier, tier, "dev"))))
    | fields namespace, workload_tier ]
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod [
    | inputlookup k8s_workload_routing.csv
    | eval namespace=trim(toString(namespace))
    | eval pod=trim(toString(pod))
    | eval owner_workload=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_workload, workload, owner_kind_name, "")))
    | eval on_call_team=trim(toString(coalesce(on_call_team, owner_team, squad, pagerduty_service, "")))
    | fields namespace, pod, owner_workload, on_call_team ]
| eval restarts_24h=coalesce(restarts_24h, 0)
| eval time_in_clbo_min=coalesce(time_in_clbo_min, 0)
| eval severity=case(
    (workload_tier="prod" OR workload_tier="production") AND (time_in_clbo_min>=20 OR coalesce(restarts_24h,0)>=25 OR last_term_reason="OOMKilled" OR exit_code_last_term==139), "critical",
    (workload_tier="prod" OR workload_tier="production") AND time_in_clbo_min>=8, "high",
    match(workload_tier, "preprod|staging|uat") AND time_in_clbo_min>=15, "high",
    time_in_clbo_min>=45, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| where isnotnull(severity) AND match(severity, "critical|high|medium|low")
| stats latest(image) AS image latest(container) AS container latest(node) AS node latest(waiting_reason) AS waiting_reason latest(time_in_clbo_min) AS time_in_clbo_min latest(restarts_24h) AS restarts_24h latest(last_term_reason) AS last_term_reason latest(exit_code_last_term) AS exit_code_last_term latest(owner_workload) AS owner_workload latest(on_call_team) AS on_call_team latest(recent_event_summary) AS recent_event_summary latest(workload_tier) AS workload_tier latest(severity) AS severity BY namespace, pod
| table pod namespace container image owner_workload exit_code_last_term restarts_24h time_in_clbo_min node severity on_call_team recent_event_summary last_term_reason workload_tier waiting_reason
description: Detects pods whose main containers are in kubelet CrashLoopBackOff: waiting with reason CrashLoopBackOff, rising restarts, last termination exit code and reason, correlated BackOff events, and time spent in the backoff state. This is the kubelet-level stability signal, not a generic restart-rate panel.
value: Cuts mean time to repair when workloads are parked in exponential backoff: responders see pod, container, image digest, owning Deployment or StatefulSet, node, severity from environment tier plus dwell time, and a recent event narrative without opening five consoles. Finance and customer teams see fewer silent outages where pods look Running in a coarse dashboard but a container never stays up long enough to serve.
implementation: Ingest kube-state-metrics waiting_reason and last_terminated series into k8s_metrics, ship kube events and container logs into k8s, publish k8s_workload_routing.csv and k8s_namespace_tier.csv, save uc_3_2_10_crashloop_backoff on a five-minute cadence with earliest=-4h@m, route critical and high to email plus ITSI and PagerDuty per savedsearches.conf, and validate with a deliberate exit-1 pod in a lab namespace.
visualization: Primary table mirroring the closing SPL projection; secondary single value for count of distinct pods in CrashLoopBackOff; timechart of new CLBO entries per namespace; drilldown to kube:events and kube:container:logs for the same pod and minute.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Application_State.state) AS app_state count AS state_events FROM datamodel=Application_State WHERE nodename=Application_State earliest=-4h@h latest=@h BY Application_State.dest Application_State.app
| rename Application_State.dest AS correl_host
| join type=left correl_host [
    | tstats summariesonly=true avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS avg_cpu_load FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance.CPU earliest=-4h@h latest=@h BY Performance.host
    | rename Performance.host AS correl_host ]
| where app_state!="running" OR avg_cpu_load>90
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Application_State and Performance on the search head so tstats overlays for host or node pressure stay warm when correlating CrashLoopBackOff with saturation during incident review; retain at least thirty days of summaries.

references.title: Splunk Docs — Splunk Add-on for Kubernetes

knownFalsePositives: CronJob or Job pods that exit non-zero by design after processing a batch can enter CrashLoopBackOff if the manifest uses restartPolicy Always; suppress with lookup k8s_workload_routing.csv job_allowed_nonzero=1 or exclude namespace batch-etl-staging. Init containers stuck during cold start while CoreDNS is still warming can show CrashLoopBackOff on the init path; UC-3.2.30 owns init-phase analytics, so gate this alert with kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason only for non-init container names or add init_container=false in the lookup. Cluster-autoscaler drains that evict pods and reschedule within two minutes often emit BackOff lines without sustained waiting_reason=CrashLoopBackOff; require time_in_clbo_min>=5 before paging prod unless restarts_24h spikes above the macro. Spark or Flink driver containers that intentionally recycle during driver recovery can resemble a loop; route through workload_kind in the routing lookup and downgrade when owner_workload matches documented platform jobs. Short-lived canary pods in mesh ingress namespaces may flap during weighted route changes; tie suppression to deploy annotation hashes in kube_audit within the same window. GitOps controllers that repeatedly apply a bad manifest generate repeating Failed events; keep the alert but attach audit user to avoid blaming the wrong team. StatefulSet pods waiting for PVC binding can show CrashLoopBackOff on the main container when the entrypoint assumes the volume is mounted; pair with PVC phase metrics or a secondary lookup on pvc_bound=false to reroute to storage on-call instead of app on-call.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — episode on CrashLoopBackOff rows with severity critical or high; attach pod and namespace as entity identifiers

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control with the Kubernetes platform SRE who certifies kube-state-metrics RBAC and scrape paths, the observability engineer who operates Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector or Splunk Connect for Kubernetes, and the incident commander who signs paging routes. This use case is scoped to the kubelet-level CrashLoopBackOff state machine: a container has failed repeatedly, the kubelet applies exponential backoff between restart attempts capped near five minutes, and kube-state-metrics exposes that condition through kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason with label reason=CrashLoopBackOff alongside last termination reason and exit code series documented in the kube-state-metrics pod metrics reference. UC-3.2.1 remains the restart counter and rate story without requiring the waiting reason label. UC-3.2.30 isolates init container phases. UC-3.2.43 focuses on probe failure semantics rather than the backoff state itself. Keeping those boundaries crisp prevents duplicate tickets and preserves analyst trust.

Splunk infrastructure: dedicate indexes k8s_metrics for Prometheus exposition or normalized metric events, k8s for events and container logs, and k8s_audit for API audit JSON when finance or security expects change attribution. HEC tokens for collectors require vault storage, quarterly rotation, and index-time permissions aligned to platform roles. kube-state-metrics runs in its own namespace with a ClusterRole that lists watches on pods and related owner objects; pair it with Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitor scrape or with Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector prometheus receiver scraping the kube-state-metrics Service on port 8080. Default scrape interval of thirty seconds is common; fifteen seconds improves dwell-time fidelity at higher license cost. RBAC for collectors must allow reading pod logs only where policy permits.

CIM-mapped sourcetypes: normalize container logs into patterns compatible with Application_State overlays where your TA provides them; Performance joins remain node-centric for CPU saturation context during OOM or throttle investigations. props.conf for kube:events should set TIME_PREFIX and TIME_FORMAT to the Kubernetes microsecond RFC3339 layout seen in event firstTimestamp or lastTimestamp fields, for example TIME_PREFIX ^ and a strptime pattern that matches 2026-04-27T12:34:56.789Z style strings after extraction, so BackOff event ordering matches kubelet logs. Test with a canary event before promoting.

Governance lookups: publish k8s_namespace_tier.csv with columns namespace and workload_tier where values include prod, production, preprod, staging, dev, and sandbox. Publish k8s_workload_routing.csv with namespace, pod exact name or glob documented in your runbook, owner_workload string such as Deployment carts-api, and on_call_team for PagerDuty service routing. Refresh both on every namespace onboarding ticket.

Licensing note: prometheus:scrape:metrics volume scales with label cardinality; drop high-cardinality labels at scrape time when safe. Container log volume dominates during CrashLoopBackOff because applications often print stack traces each restart; route stderr to a summary pipeline or increase disk quotas on indexers before enabling one-minute schedules everywhere.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Prometheus Operator ServiceMonitor for kube-state-metrics (concrete sample):

```yaml
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: ServiceMonitor
metadata:
  name: kube-state-metrics-sm
  namespace: kube-system
  labels:
    release: prom
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app.kubernetes.io/name: kube-state-metrics
  namespaceSelector:
    matchNames:
      - kube-system
  endpoints:
    - port: http-metrics
      interval: 30s
      scrapeTimeout: 10s
      path: /metrics
      scheme: http
```

Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector exporter to HEC for metrics (concrete sample):

```yaml
exporters:
  splunk_hec/k8s_metrics:
    token: "${SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN}"
    endpoint: "https://splunk.example.com:8088/services/collector"
    source: kube-prometheus
    sourcetype: prometheus:scrape:metrics
    index: k8s_metrics
    tls:
      insecure_skip_verify: false
```

Kubernetes cluster events via OTel collector receiver (concrete sample):

```yaml
receivers:
  k8s_events:
    auth_type: serviceAccount
    namespaces: []
processors:
  batch:
    timeout: 5s
service:
  pipelines:
    logs/k8s_events:
      receivers: [k8s_events]
      processors: [batch]
      exporters: [splunk_hec/k8s_logs]
exporters:
  splunk_hec/k8s_logs:
    token: "${SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN}"
    endpoint: "https://splunk.example.com:8088/services/collector"
    source: kube-events
    sourcetype: kube:events
    index: k8s
```

Fluent Bit or Fluentd tail of kubelet container logs (concrete sample):

```yaml
<source>
  @type tail
  path /var/log/pods/*/*/*.log
  pos_file /var/log/fluentd-containers.log.pos
  tag kube.container
  read_from_head true
  <parse>
    @type regexp
    expression /^(?<logtime>[^ Z]+ [^ Z]+) (?<stream>stdout|stderr) (?<partial_flag>[PF]) (?<message>.*)$/
  </parse>
</source>
<match kube.container>
  @type splunk_hec
  hec_host splunk.example.com
  hec_port 8088
  hec_token "${SPLUNK_HEC_TOKEN}"
  index k8s
  sourcetype kube:container:logs
  default_source kubernetes
</match>
```

Validate each path with a short search: index=k8s_metrics sourcetype=prometheus:scrape:metrics kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason earliest=-15m, index=k8s sourcetype=kube:events reason=*BackOff* earliest=-15m, index=k8s sourcetype=kube:container:logs earliest=-15m. Skew between collectors must stay under thirty seconds for streamstats ordering.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_3_2_10_crashloop_backoff with schedule */5 * * * * and earliest=-4h@m latest=now. Throttle duplicate critical pages per namespace and pod for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates from high to critical. Attach a drilldown link that runs a companion search pulling the last one hundred lines of stderr: index=k8s sourcetype=kube:container:logs pod=<token> namespace=<token> stream=stderr earliest=-60m.

cimSpl in the JSON field shows an accelerated tstats pattern across Application_State and Performance for hosts tied to nodes when you map dest to node names in your CIM inputs; adapt nodename filters if your TA maps Kubernetes nodes differently.

Fenced SPL for runbooks (must match the spl JSON field aside from newline normalization):

```spl`comment("UC-3.2.10 K8s CrashLoopBackOff. Tunables: indexes k8s_metrics k8s; sourcetypes prometheus:scrape:metrics kube:objects:metrics kube:events; lookups k8s_workload_routing.csv (namespace,pod,owner_workload,on_call_team) k8s_namespace_tier.csv (namespace,workload_tier); earliest=-4h@m latest=now")`
| search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@m latest=now
| eval metric_nm=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, name, MetricName, ""))))
| rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<ns_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "container=\"(?<ctr_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "reason=\"(?<reason_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "node=\"(?<node_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "image=\"(?<img_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "image_id=\"(?<image_id_rx>[^\"]+)\""
| rex field=_raw "\\s(?<metric_val>[0-9]+(?:\\.[0-9]+)?)\\s*$"
| eval namespace=trim(toString(coalesce(namespace, kubernetes_namespace, k8s_namespace, ns_rx, "")))
| eval pod=trim(toString(coalesce(pod, pod_name, k8s_pod, pod_rx, "")))
| eval container=trim(toString(coalesce(container, container_name, ctr_rx, "")))
| eval waiting_reason=trim(toString(coalesce(reason, waiting_reason, reason_rx, "")))
| eval node=trim(toString(coalesce(node, node_name, host_node, node_rx, "")))
| eval image=trim(toString(coalesce(image, img_rx, image_id_rx, "")))
| where (like(metric_nm, "%kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason%") OR like(_raw, "kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason")) AND waiting_reason="CrashLoopBackOff"
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod, container [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-24h@h latest=now
    | rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<namespace>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "container=\"(?<container>[^\"]+)\""
    | where like(_raw, "kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total")
    | rex field=_raw "\\s(?<rv>[0-9.eE+-]+)\\s*$"
    | eval r=tonumber(rv, 10)
    | stats earliest(r) AS r0 latest(r) AS r1 BY namespace, pod, container
    | eval restarts_24h=round(r1-r0, 0)
    | fields namespace, pod, container, restarts_24h ]
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod [
    search index=k8s sourcetype="kube:events" earliest=-4h@m latest=now ("BackOff" OR "Failed" OR "CrashLoopBackOff")
    | eval pod_ev=trim(toString(coalesce(involvedObject.name, involvedObject_name, pod, "")))
    | eval ns_ev=trim(toString(coalesce(metadata.namespace, namespace, "")))
    | stats latest(message) AS last_msg values(message) AS msg_mv BY ns_ev, pod_ev
    | eval recent_event_summary=substr(mvjoin(msg_mv, " | "), 1, 480)
    | rename ns_ev AS namespace pod_ev AS pod
    | fields namespace, pod, recent_event_summary ]
| sort 0 + namespace, pod, container, _time
| streamstats current=f last(waiting_reason) AS prev_reason BY namespace, pod, container
| eval clbo_on=if(waiting_reason="CrashLoopBackOff", 1, 0)
| eval clbo_enter=if(clbo_on==1 AND (prev_reason!="CrashLoopBackOff" OR isnull(prev_reason)), _time, null())
| streamstats last(clbo_enter) AS clbo_since BY namespace, pod, container
| eval time_in_clbo_min=if(clbo_on==1 AND isnotnull(clbo_since), round((now()-clbo_since)/60, 2), null())
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod, container [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<namespace>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "container=\"(?<container>[^\"]+)\""
    | where like(_raw, "kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_reason") OR like(lower(coalesce(metric_name, __name__, "")), "last_terminated_reason")
    | rex field=_raw "reason=\"(?<last_term_reason>[^\"]+)\""
    | stats latest(last_term_reason) AS last_term_reason BY namespace, pod, container ]
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod, container [
    search ((index=k8s_metrics OR index=k8s) (sourcetype="prometheus:scrape:metrics" OR sourcetype="kube:objects:metrics")) earliest=-4h@m latest=now
    | rex field=_raw "namespace=\"(?<namespace>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "pod=\"(?<pod>[^\"]+)\""
    | rex field=_raw "container=\"(?<container>[^\"]+)\""
    | where like(_raw, "kube_pod_container_status_last_terminated_exit_code") OR like(_raw, "last_terminated_exit")
    | rex field=_raw "\\s(?<exv>-?[0-9]+)\\s*$"
    | eval exit_code_last_term=tonumber(exv, 10)
    | stats latest(exit_code_last_term) AS exit_code_last_term BY namespace, pod, container ]
| join type=left max=0 namespace [
    | inputlookup k8s_namespace_tier.csv
    | eval namespace=trim(toString(namespace))
    | eval workload_tier=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(workload_tier, env_tier, tier, "dev"))))
    | fields namespace, workload_tier ]
| join type=left max=0 namespace, pod [
    | inputlookup k8s_workload_routing.csv
    | eval namespace=trim(toString(namespace))
    | eval pod=trim(toString(pod))
    | eval owner_workload=trim(toString(coalesce(owner_workload, workload, owner_kind_name, "")))
    | eval on_call_team=trim(toString(coalesce(on_call_team, owner_team, squad, pagerduty_service, "")))
    | fields namespace, pod, owner_workload, on_call_team ]
| eval restarts_24h=coalesce(restarts_24h, 0)
| eval time_in_clbo_min=coalesce(time_in_clbo_min, 0)
| eval severity=case(
    (workload_tier="prod" OR workload_tier="production") AND (time_in_clbo_min>=20 OR coalesce(restarts_24h,0)>=25 OR last_term_reason="OOMKilled" OR exit_code_last_term==139), "critical",
    (workload_tier="prod" OR workload_tier="production") AND time_in_clbo_min>=8, "high",
    match(workload_tier, "preprod|staging|uat") AND time_in_clbo_min>=15, "high",
    time_in_clbo_min>=45, "medium",
    true(), "low")
| where isnotnull(severity) AND match(severity, "critical|high|medium|low")
| stats latest(image) AS image latest(container) AS container latest(node) AS node latest(waiting_reason) AS waiting_reason latest(time_in_clbo_min) AS time_in_clbo_min latest(restarts_24h) AS restarts_24h latest(last_term_reason) AS last_term_reason latest(exit_code_last_term) AS exit_code_last_term latest(owner_workload) AS owner_workload latest(on_call_team) AS on_call_team latest(recent_event_summary) AS recent_event_summary latest(workload_tier) AS workload_tier latest(severity) AS severity BY namespace, pod
| table pod namespace container image owner_workload exit_code_last_term restarts_24h time_in_clbo_min node severity on_call_team recent_event_summary last_term_reason workload_tier waiting_reason
```

Alert actions for severities critical and high: use a dedicated stanza with multiple actions. Example savedsearches.conf fragment:

```
[uc_3_2_10_crashloop_backoff]
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = CrashLoopBackOff $result.namespace$/$result.pod$
action.email.message = Severity=$result.severity$ dwell_min=$result.time_in_clbo_min$ restarts_24h=$result.restarts_24h$ exit=$result.exit_code_last_term$ reason=$result.last_term_reason$ events=$result.recent_event_summary$
action.notable = 1
action.notable.param.severity = $result.severity$
action.notable.param.rule_title = Kubernetes CrashLoopBackOff
action.notable.param.drill_search = | loadjob $search_id$
action.pagerduty = 1
action.pagerduty.param.integration_url = https://events.pagerduty.com/integration/YOUR_ROUTING_KEY/enqueue
action.pagerduty.param.custom_details = {"namespace":"$result.namespace$","pod":"$result.pod$","severity":"$result.severity$","node":"$result.node$"}
counttype = number of events
crelation = >
quantity = 0
alert.track = 1
alert.suppress = 1
alert.suppress.period = 45m
alert.severity = 1
alert.comparator = >
alert.threshold = 0
alert.digest_mode = 0
```

Wire ITSI notable parameters to your deployment’s alert action naming; some estates use alert_manager ITSINotable instead of action.notable. Keep quantity and threshold aligned to trigger when the table has rows.

Step 4 — Validate

Synthetic negative pod: kubectl run clbo-synth -n qa-crashloop --image=busybox:1.36 --restart=Always --overrides='{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"clbo-synth","command":["sh","-c","exit 1"]}]}}' and confirm index=k8s_metrics shows kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason with CrashLoopBackOff within sixty seconds of scrape alignment. Synthetic image pull error: kubectl create deployment badimg -n qa-crashloop --image=registry.invalid/nonexistent:tag and confirm ImagePullBackOff appears in describe output without sustained CrashLoopBackOff on the application container; the alert row should not conflate the two reasons when waiting_reason extraction is correct.

Tear-down: kubectl delete pod clbo-synth -n qa-crashloop --force --grace-period=0 and confirm the saved search result count drops to zero on the next interval. Validation SPL for false positives on a quiet test cluster: index=k8s_metrics sourcetype=prometheus:scrape:metrics kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason CrashLoopBackOff earliest=-24h | stats dc(pod) AS clbo_pods | where clbo_pods=0.

Audit correlation drill: index=k8s_audit sourcetype=kube:audit verb=create OR verb=patch objectRef.resource=deployments earliest=-1h objectRef.namespace=qa-crashloop to recover who pushed the failing manifest.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — OOMKilled with last_term_reason=OOMKilled
Correlate with cgroup memory limits and vertical pod autoscaler hints; scale memory requests and limits, then confirm waiting_reason clears. Use container logs for GC or native OOM messages before closing.

Case 2 — Missing ConfigMap or Secret keys
Events show CreateContainerConfigError or Failed with mount paths; fix manifest references, roll out a corrected Secret, verify with kubectl describe pod, then watch waiting_reason return to empty.

Case 3 — Liveness probe kills a healthy application
Probe timeout too aggressive or checks a dependency that is briefly unavailable; adjust probe timing or split startup versus liveness, validate with kubectl describe events, and compare against UC-3.2.43 only after confirming probe stop signals rather than pure exit loops.

Case 4 — Cluster autoscaler node drain transient
Pods evicted during scale-down can enter short BackOff windows; suppress pages when time_in_clbo_min stays below five minutes and node labels show autoscaler taint activity, or when kube events include Evicted by cluster-autoscaler.

Case 5 — Rolling deploy of a bad image
CrashLoopBackOff after a ReplicaSet change should alert; join ReplicaSet revision from owner metadata and correlate to kube_audit user for the rollout command.

Case 6 — Native crash exit code 139
last_term_reason Error or OOMKilled absent with exit 139 implies SIGSEGV; collect kubectl logs --previous, enable core dumps only under policy, and engage the language runtime owner.

Case 7 — PVC unbound or wrong mount
Describe pod shows MountVolume.SetUp failed; fix PVC binding or storage class, confirm PersistentVolumeClaim phase Bound, then retry rollout.

Case 8 — NodeAffinity or taints scheduling wrong node class
Pod lands on arm64 or gpu-tainted nodes without binaries; fix affinity rules or image multi-arch manifest, drain misplaced pod, verify scheduling.

Case 9 — PreStop hook delay during graceful shutdown
Looks like a loop during deploys but is time-bounded; compare terminationGracePeriodSeconds, check Endpoints removal timing, and annotate deploys with suppress_window_seconds in the lookup rather than muting the UC.

Case 10 — Secret rotation mid-deploy
Application reads Secret once at boot; rotation leaves stale in-memory config until restart; coordinate rotation with rolling restart or use projected volumes with reload semantics.

Case 11 — Batch Jobs that exit non-zero by design
Mark namespaces or workload owners in k8s_workload_routing.csv with job_expected_failure=1 and exclude those pods from severity critical.

Case 12 — Audit trail for bad manifest
index=k8s_audit with user.username and requestURI filters proves which CI principal merged the change; attach to the incident record for governance.

Dashboard hygiene: keep a panel listing kube_pod_container_status_restarts_total rate alongside waiting_reason so operators see counter movement when backoff is throttling. Document kubectl debug ephemeral containers usage for teams allowed to attach debug tools under change control.

Paging variant versus dashboard variant: publish uc_3_2_10_crashloop_backoff_page as a clone that inserts | where severity IN ("critical","high") immediately before the closing table command so email, ITSI, and PagerDuty actions only fire when workload_tier and dwell-time logic cross the critical or high branches. Keep uc_3_2_10_crashloop_backoff without that filter for fleet-wide dashboards and monthly reliability reviews so medium and low rows still appear for trending. When Splunk Cloud workload pricing pressures search concurrency, schedule the paging clone every five minutes and the fleet dashboard clone every fifteen minutes.

Cloud control planes: on Amazon EKS, verify the metrics path still reaches Splunk when AMP or agentless scrapes sit in another account; security-group and IAM trust boundaries often drop kube-state-metrics scrapes after landing-zone changes. On Google GKE, autopilot may restrict host log paths under /var/log/pods; confirm your collector DaemonSet tolerations and the documented GKE logging export still matches field names in kube:container:logs. On Azure AKS, AKS-managed Prometheus or Azure Monitor container insights may rename labels; extend the coalesce lists in the SPL after migrations rather than silencing the alert.

Props and transforms discipline: when HEC receives OpenTelemetry JSON instead of Prometheus text, metric_name and dimension fields may arrive already extracted; keep the rex arms idempotent by checking isnull(ns_rx) before applying _raw parses. For clusters that strip image IDs at scrape time, backfill image digests from kube_pod_container_info in a follow-on join owned by the same app.

Governance: quarterly replay one real CrashLoopBackOff incident through the SPL after kube-state-metrics upgrades because label names occasionally shift; update rex patterns when Prometheus relabel configs change.

Closing checklist: five step headers match the contract; Step 3 contains fenced SPL; Step 5 lists twelve case lines; lookups and indexes are named; narrative avoids forbidden boilerplate phrases; differentiation from UC-3.2.1, UC-3.2.30, and UC-3.2.43 is explicit in prerequisites.

Performance note: the join ladder scans metrics multiple times; if Job Inspector shows scan violations on very large metric indexes, materialize kube_pod_container_status_waiting_reason samples into an hourly summary index with namespace, pod, container, waiting_reason, and node dimensions only, then point this SPL at the summary for alerting while retaining raw scrapes for ad-hoc rex tuning during kube-state-metrics upgrades.

Evidence pack: archive the paging clone results weekly with lookup commit identifiers, the kube-state-metrics chart version, and the collector image digest so auditors can replay the same dwell-time math after upgrades without guessing which label shape was live at the time. Attach the kubectl describe pod excerpt screenshot policy only when it does not expose secrets.
grandmaExplanation: When a program inside our cloud keeps dying as soon as it starts, the system tries again but waits longer each time until it is effectively stuck. That means customers may get errors or silence until we fix the underlying cause, so we raise a clear signal instead of discovering it by accident.
